item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would you eat it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should this have the "food" tag?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm fairly indifferent towards mayonnaise, and this is a by the numbers parasitic-normal-thing (and/or parasitic foodstuff) article, with an okay note tacked at the end for reasons I'm ambivalent on (I guess we do not look at our food's label, but then again why would a parasite announce its presencce. Are these extra-courteous body invaders?).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hate mayo and this doesn't help. 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I knew mayo was demon spawn! +1

item.title: Staff Post - Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Revision 9 of this article has been reverted to Revision 10, as it corrected intentional typos. That being said, it'd probably help to add something like [sic] after each typo to show that it's intentional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was the exact line I was going to cite for the moment this article won me over.

Seriously, this is incredibly clever. Also incredibly gross.

Bravo, +1.

item.title: Staff Post: Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Version 6 of this article has been reverted, as it made content changes without permission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2484-A is able to be destroyed by any means applicable to non-anomalous mayonnaise.

This has to be the most ridiculous line of text from any main series SCP that I've read.

Overall, I'm really digging this item. Even though it's something that invariably kills someone who eats too much of it, it's not so over the top that it becomes unbelievable. There's some good humor there too, but it doesn't distract from the danger. This is one I'd be glad to see stay on the main list for good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept didnt get my attention, but the tone is good and some parts have cool hooks to them. Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What mist said. And the Foundation doesn't want to accidentally break the jar because they don't now what the contents would do in that situation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They eat you. Then they eat each other. Then the survivor goes dormant. No idea why.

2484 is the jar. So we don't do destructive testing on the jar. "Secure, Contain, Protect", not "Smash, Crush, Pummel".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A little author insight
close

The idea is that this is a species from somewhere, that reached a point in their evolution where they were no longer able to reproduce among themselves, and so in order to live at all, they did something which resulted in them being able to be birthed from mayo. I do actually have a whole backstory for these critters, but it didn't really work out, so it got cut. Maybe it'll show up in a tale some day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the screwy label, but that final worm that just dies still seems like a total letdown to me. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just assumed the misspellings were intentional on the author's part. It's an alien intelligence, after all.

EAT. EGGS. BE. EGGS.

That's not the voice of someone who's thinking the way we might.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the text box I think
"Eggs. EGGS. EAT. EGGS. BE. EGGS. YOURE FLESH IS BUT THE NURSERY AND SUSTINENCE OF OUR INCALCULABLE RAVENOUS MOUTHS."
is supposed to be
"Eggs. EGGS. EAT. EGGS. BE. EGGS. YOUR FLESH IS BUT THE NURSERY AND SUSTENANCE OF OUR INCALCULABLE RAVENOUS MOUTHS."

Good work, very fun.
+1

item.title: Issues
item.content:encoded:
I quite like this, but I think 2 issues should be addressed:

The picture looks like the plastic wrap is still on the jar, but that should not be the case. I think a picture adds to the article, but this specific picture looks like a jar that has not been opened.

that possesses numerous anomalous qualities that begin to manifest when the substance is removed from the jar and consumed by a vertebrate animal

To me this statement implies that the anomalous qualities do not start until consumed, but that is not the case.

-

Overall I found this to be disturbing and well written. The ingredients, and the "non-anomalous mayonnaise" comment are pretty nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, to start +1. I love it.

But what happens after the worms develop? They go dormant, why? And why are destructive tests not allowed? Did I miss the reason?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it, but I have one question (Three questions) . How was the label modified by the contents of the jar? Is it implying this was created by the species (possibly adults)? Is it some extradimensional gag gift? Definitely a +1 Even though I happen to like mayonnaise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I always knew mayonnaise was evil.

+1 for body horror and screaming forever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not like I haven't seen something like this before, but I had to upvote for the label as well as

SCP-2484-A is able to be destroyed by any means applicable to non-anomalous mayonnaise.

I mean, just "non-anomalous mayonnaise" by itself is hilarious. That said, the first sentence of the description should be cut in two, it's pretty unwieldy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I liked most of it, a lot, but I did think the final note was a bit ham-fisted, personally. (No pun intended) Not enough to keep from an up vote, however. Great job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Makes me even more disgusted by mayonnaise. I am impressed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Contrary to the others, I am not wowed by the ingredient list (it feels anti-climatic and in a bad way, while coming off as arbitrary). The initial bunch is still alright with me. I will really have to call it a neutral at this point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a rather formulaic item + explanatory note article, but the ingredients list is cute, I'll give you that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was lucky enough to see it linked in chat, and enjoyed it then. The addition of the ingredients list was a delightful surprise. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Creepy, strange and quite disgusting, definitely one to make people look at mayonnaise and kill it with fire. Love the effort and thought into this SCP, making a jar of mayonnaise into a mass of deadly parasites that want to eat your flesh.

Water, vegetable oil, vinegar, eggs. Eggs. EGGS. EAT. EGGS. BE. EGGS. YOURE FLESH IS BUT THE NURSERY AND SUSTINENCE OF OUR INCALCULABLE RAVENOUS MOUTHS. EAT EGGS. CONSUME US. LET US BE REALIZED WITHIN YOU, sugar, salt, lemon juice, and love.

This bit here secured my vote. Maybe it's just a personnel like of mine, but I love disease/biological SCPs that have abilities like this. I don't really know what to criticise, you put this on the forums and it seems refined and well made as a "gross out" article as well as the fact that there is no grammar and spelling mistakes as far as I can see.

Some may disagree with me, but I think this is original and clever, also catching my attention from the get-go, making me think "I wonder what this jar of mayonnaise does?" Nice job on this Dr Solo. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to the dozens of people who helped in the chat, and the few on the forum.
Image edit credit to AJMansfield
Written for the tumblr crew.
